---
title: "Exercise 4B: Data Agent"
subtitle: "MGMT 675: Generative AI for Finance"
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \PassOptionsToPackage{colorlinks=true,urlcolor=blue,linkcolor=blue}{hyperref}
---

Build a chatbot that lets users query an M\&A transaction database in plain English.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Background.}
Download \href{https://mgmt675.kerryback.com/files/manda.db}{manda.db}, a SQLite database of 200 M\&A transactions (2019--2025) with three tables: \texttt{transactions}, \texttt{advisors}, and \texttt{financing}.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Step 1: Explore the database.}
Place \texttt{manda.db} in a project folder and ask Claude Code:

\begin{quote}
\textit{Read the SQLite database manda.db.  Describe each table's columns and data types, and show the first three rows of each table.}
\end{quote}

Review the output so you understand the schema before proceeding.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Step 2: Build the chatbot.}
Ask Claude Code to build a FastAPI chatbot app that:

\begin{enumerate}
  \item Connects to \texttt{manda.db} and answers user questions by generating and executing SQL queries.
  \item Uses the OpenRouter API with a free model (e.g., \texttt{nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free}).
  \item Includes a system prompt that describes the database schema, column meanings, and any data conventions (e.g., deal values are in millions, some fields may be NULL).
\end{enumerate}

The app should ensure all required libraries are installed before trying to import them.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Step 3: Test the chatbot.}
Test with at least 5 questions, for example:

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textit{What are the five largest completed deals?}
  \item \textit{Which banks advised the most deals as target advisor?}
  \item \textit{What is the average EV/EBITDA multiple for tech acquisitions?}
  \item \textit{Show me all hostile deals.}
  \item \textit{How much debt financing did LBOs use on average?}
\end{itemize}

Evaluate whether the chatbot generates correct SQL and returns sensible answers.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Deliverables.}

\begin{itemize}
  \item Code (\texttt{4B-Code.zip}) --- include \texttt{manda.db} and your FastAPI app
  \item Screenshot of the running app in your browser showing at least 3 query--response exchanges (\texttt{4B-Screenshot.png})
  \item Evaluation of answer quality (\texttt{4B-Evaluation.pdf}, half page)
\end{itemize}
